title: carlina × bakariensis
intro: Carlina × bakariensis is a hybrid plant species obtained through cross-pollination between Carlina acaulis and Carlina gummifer. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Carlina × bakariensis include its ability to relieve pain, reduce fever, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for various forms of administration, including topical applications for skin conditions and internal consumption for overall well-being.
